REQUEST FOR CLARIFICATION OF REPORTING REQUIREITIENTS
Dear Mr. Hallibrin:
My campaign has received conflicting informat,ion from the FPPC
concerning reporting requirements. This let,ter is to request
clarification 
of your reguirements.
I am mailing a letter to my constituents outlining my positions
and recommendations on l-3 1ocal and state measures and
candidates. My campaign paid approximately $2400 for the desS-gn,
printing and postage of this piece.
We are unclear as to how this is reported to the FPPC. We have
been told that lve should apportion the cost, among the various
campaigns, based on the amount of space they received, and this
is what we have done. Each candidate and measure has been
informed of the amount spent on their behalf so they can meet any
of their reporting requirements.
Howeverr w€ have also been told to report it as though each
candidate and balIot measure received a $2400 contribution from
my campaign, and that each campaign would report it as a $2400
donation. If the second opinion hoLds, it would aPpear as though
r spent upwards of $30r000 instead of the 52400 actually expendeci
and that each candidate and issue received a $2400 donation.
I believe that the way I have report,ed these expendi,tures is
correct. If your review of this situation indicates otherwise,
please let me kpow immediately so that we can make any
adjustments to meet, your requirements.
My campaign office number is 510-849-1986. Thank you for your
assistance in this matter.
Sincerely,

sections.question: My campaign has received conflicting information from the FPPC concerning reporting requirements. I am mailing a letter to my constituents outlining my positions and recommendations on 1-3 local and state measures and candidates. My campaign paid approximately $2400 for the design, printing and postage of this piece. We are unclear as to how this is reported to the FPPC. We have been told that we should apportion the cost among the various campaigns, based on the amount of space they received, and this is what we have done. Each candidate and measure has been informed of the amount spent on their behalf so they can meet any of their reporting requirements. However, we have also been told to report it as though each candidate and ballot measure received a $2400 contribution from my campaign, and that each campaign would report it as a $2400 donation. Which approach is correct?
sections.question_synthetic: How should a campaign allocate and report the costs of a multi-candidate/measure mailing piece—by apportioning the total cost among recipients based on space received, or by reporting the full cost as a separate contribution to each candidate/measure?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Tom Bates to FPPC General Counsel, not an FPPC response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present, but the request content is clearly stated in the body. OCR quality is poor in places but core content is legible. Document dated May 22, 1992.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: My campaign has received conflicting information from the FPPC concerning reporting requirements. I am mailing a letter to my constituents outlining my positions and recommendations on 1-3 local and state measures and candidates. My campaign paid approximately $2400 for the design, printing and postage of this piece. We are unclear as to how this is reported to the FPPC. We have been told that we should apportion the cost among the various campaigns, based on the amount of space they received, and this is what we have done. Each candidate and measure has been informed of the amount spent on their behalf so they can meet any of their reporting requirements. However, we have also been told to report it as though each candidate and ballot measure received a $2400 contribution from my campaign, and that each campaign would report it as a $2400 donation. Which approach is correct?

embedding.summary: California Assembly Member Tom Bates requests FPPC clarification on proper reporting of a $2400 multi-candidate/measure mailing expense, asking whether to apportion costs or report the full amount as a contribution to each recipient.
